answer:
In Cynewulf's *Juliana*, the prison scene (or dungeon) unfolds after Juliana is tortured and confined. A demon disguised as an angel enters her cell to tempt her to sacrifice to idols; she seizes and binds him on divine command, then interrogates him at length about his origins, mission, methods of temptation, and past evils (including against biblical figures like Christ, Peter, Paul, and Andrew). He confesses reluctantly under duress.[^1]

Juliana's key speeches during the confrontation include:

> She asked him then boldly, she who was not afraid,  
> she who was pleasing to Christ, from where he had come.[^2]

> The holy one answered him, through the Spirit’s gift,  
> Juliana: “You must tell me further,  
> fiend of mankind, of your journey,  
> and who sent you to me.”[^3]

> Then the holy one began again to question  
> the enemy of heroes, the worker of ruin,  
> the source of ancient sins: “You must tell me further,  
> fiend of souls, how you most harm the righteous  
> through the slide of sin, you who are steeped in deceit.”[^4]

> Then the maiden spoke again: “Tell me, wretched creature,  
> unclean spirit, how you, ruler of darkness,  
> dare to join the company of the clean?  
> You once strove against Christ, faithless one,  
> and waged war, plotted against the holy.  
> The pit of hell was dug deep for you,  
> where you, in your pride, sought a dwelling in distress.  
> I thought you would have become more wary  
> of such meetings with the righteous, and less bold,  
> you who have often been thwarted by the King of glory.”[^5]

> Then the fair-shining candle of glory  
> spoke these words to the oath-breaker:  
> “You must confess more of your evil deeds,  
> lowly hell-spirit, before you may go from here,  
> what great and wicked works you have wrought  
> to the ruin of mankind with your dark delusions.”[^6]

She later drags the bound demon before her tormentors.[^7]

[^1]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [ch. 1](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/), paragraphs [p-89a6c9](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-89a6c9) through [p-2309da](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-2309da).
[^2]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-feeb82).
[^3]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-bd327c).
[^4]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-def3ab).
[^5]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-579347).
[^6]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-d3a506).
[^7]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-6b7e11).
